key_insight: Defect-free FeS2(100) terraces can host two distinct L-cysteine supramolecular assemblies, including a compact ordered phase and a more labile phase driven by trimer-based interactions.
engineering_relevance: This shows that pristine mineral surfaces, not just defect sites, can guide molecular self-organization and could inform the design of catalytic or templating interfaces for prebiotic chemistry and surface-based synthesis.
method: STM/STS imaging and spectroscopy on atomically ordered pyrite terraces
DFT calculations to interpret adsorption and intermolecular interactions
Comparison of two L-cysteine supramolecular phases on defect-free FeS2(100)
evidence_signal: The abstract reports direct experimental observation of two surface phases on defectless terraces, supported by DFT identification of trimer-mediated interaction motifs.
watch_item: The assemblies are described as labile and tied to idealized defect-free terraces, so behavior on realistic, defect-rich mineral surfaces may differ.
paper_title: Prebiotic Chemistry Assemblies of L-Cysteine on Defect-Free Pyrite Terraces
